content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
April 17, L990
Janice Heidt
city councilmember
city of santa clarita
23920 valencia BIvd., suite 300
Santa C1arita, cA 913 55
Re: :::'"li:";:: i$,iili""
Dear lils . Heidt:
You are seeking advice regarding your duties and responsi-
bilities 
as a menber of the santa clarita city Council under the
confl ict-of-interest 
provisj,ons of the Political Reform Act (the
rraqS'r1.1 The following advice is based upon the facts provided in
your letter of March 26, 7990 as well as facts provided by tele-
phone on March 30, 1990 fron llr. Doug Holland, an attorney autho-
rized by you to discuss the matter with this agency.
The advice provided herein is linited to future actions only;
the Connission does not provide advj.ce with respect to past con-
duct. 
(Regulation 18329(b) (8) (A), 18329(c) (4) (A).)
OUESTION
The council wiLl be voting on a developer agreement on
April 17, 1990. The developer of a tract proposes to give the
city a portion of the tract as a right of way to enable the city
to build a road, in exchange for the city's approval of other
separate projects in other portions of the city. 
The tract, but
not the other projects, is located in the vicinity of a parcel of
commercially zoned property on which your husband owns an option.
May you participate in decisions regarding the developer agree-
ment?
1 Go.r".rrrant Code Sections 81OOO-91015. A11 statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Conmission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Sectj.on
18000, et seq. A11 references to regulations are.to Title 2, Di-
vision 6 of the California code of Regulations.
428 J street, suite 8oo . P.o. Box 8o7 o sacramento cA 958o4.o8o7 . (916)322.s66o


File No. A-9 o-245
Page 2
CONCLUSION
You may not participate in any city counciL decisions
concerning the developer agreement that will have a reasonably
foreseeable naterial financial effect on the real property on
which your husband ohrns an option. The distance betrreen the
property on which your husband has the option and the tract, the
financial irnpact of the decision on the property, and the inpact
of the decision on surrounding properties are factors that will-
detennine rrhether a foreseeable rnaterial financial effect on your
econonic interests will occur as a result of the decision.
FACTS
You are a uember of the city council of the city of Santa
C1arita. In 1988 the city council considered whether to approve
the irWest creekrr developnent in Tract #32365 (rrthe tractrt), a
project which incLuded the building of a road, Dockweiler Drive,
connecting the project to one of the nain highways of the city,
Sierra Highway.
Because your husband owned an option on a parcel of commer-
cial property in the vicinity of the tract (rroption property"),
you had requested written advice fron the Commission on whether a
conflict of interest existed that reguired your disqualification
from the tract decision. on the basis of the assj.stance provided
to you at that time, you abstained frorn participating in the dis-
cussion and decision on the tract because of a possible conflict
of interest. 
(See Newton Advice Letter, I-88-343, copy enclosed.)
The council approved the proposal without your participation, 
and
construction is currently proceeding on the tract.
The city now wishes to obtain a portion of the tract as a
right of way to construct Rio Vista Road (the rrnew roadrr) . l,lost
of the proposed ner,, road is to be located several hundred feet
north of Dockweiler Drive and also connected to Sierra Highway,
The city intends for the new road to replace Dockveiler Drive as
the roain through road for the tract. 
Although existing portions
of Dockweiler Drive will renain in their present location, these
portions will be incorporated into the new road and |tDockrreiler
Driverr will no longer exist upon the new road,s coropletion. Most
of the new road rrill be further renoved fron the option property
than the existing DockweiLer Drive, and in any event no portion
will be any closer to your husband's option property than Dock-
wei Ier Dri.ve .
The developer has proposed to give the city a portion of the
tract (trtract portiontt) for the new road in exchange for the
city,s approval of three other, unrelated projects. 
Each of these
three projects is located betrreen three and eight niles away fron
the tract.


File No. A-90-245
Page 3
The hearing for the four projects uas schedul,ed for March 13,
1990. You abstained from participating in the council's decision
on one of the three other projects; the decision on the develop-
er,s remaining two projects and the decision on the tract portion
was continued to the council meeting scheduled for April r7, L99o.
Following the hearing on llarch 13 you measured the distance
from your husband's option property to the cLosest point of the
tract, which is on the eastern portion of the tract lrhere Dock-
weiler Drive presently touches. You detemined that the distance
fron the property to the tract is approxirnately 2,900 feet.
Final.1y, you have indicated that there are more than ten Pro-
perties under separate orrnership lrithin a 2500-foot radius of the
option property.
ANALYSIS
The Act prohibits a public official 
fron making, participat-
ing in, or using his official 
position to influence a governmental
decision in which he knolrs or has reason to know he has a finan-
cial interest. 
(Section 87100.) A public official 
has a finan-
cial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that
the decision will have a rnaterial financiaf effect, distinguish-
able from its effect on the public generally, on the official 
or a
member of his irnmediate fanily. (section 87103.)
As an eLected rnember of the Santa clarita city Council, you
are a public official. 
(Section 82048.) Thus, you must disqual-
ify yourself fron participating in a city council decision which
will have a reasonably foreseeable naterial financial effect on
you or on your real property interest that is distingruishable from
the public generally.
You and Mr. Holland have indicated that there are nolr three
decisions concerning this developer pending before the council:
one concerns the tract portion and two concern other property of
the developer, Nornally, every governnental decision is indepen-
dently analyzed with respect to foreseeability and nateriality
issues. (fn re Owen (1976) 2 FPPC Ops. 77, copy enclosed.) Horr-
ever, decisions that are interrelated are not considered separate-
1y. 
(Sweenev Advice Letter, No. A-89-639; ltiller Advice Letter,
No. A-82-119, copies enclosed.) on the basis of the facts you
have provided, it would appear that decisions on the developers
two projects other than the tract portion are prerequisite and,
therefore, directly related to the council's decision on the de-
veloper agreenent to permit the city to obtain the tract portion
as a right of way to build the nes, road. If you are required to
disqualify as to the decision on the tract portion, you must dis-
qualify yourself as to the developer's other trro projects as rde1l.
(Scher Advice Letter, No. A-88-479, copy enclosed.)
An official 
has a financial interest in the decision if it
will have a foreseeable roaterial financial effect on any real
property in which the official 
has a direct or indirect interest
worth one thousand dollars (9f,ooo1 or nore. (Section 87100(b).)


File No. A-9O-245
Page 4
rrlnterest in real propertyrr means the official,s 
or his or her
spouse,s real property interests, includinlt, among other things,
an option to purchase real property. 
(Section 82033.)
Thus, you may not participate in any decision which witl have a
reasonably foreseeable naterial financial effect, distinguishable
fron its effect on the public Aenerally, on your spouse,s real
property interests.
The effect of a decision is reasonably foreseeable if there
is a substantial likelihood that it will occur. While certainty
is not required, an effect that is merely a possibility is not
reasonably foreseeable. (Downev Cares v. Downev Cornmunity Devel-
opnent Con. (1987) 195 Ca1 .App,3d 983; fn re Thorner (1975) 1 FPPC
ops. 198. )
In order to deternine whether the foreseeable effect of a
decision is rnaterial as to real property in which an official 
has
a direct, indirect or beneficial interest, the Cornroission has
adopted Regulation L8702.3 (copy enclosed).
You have infonned us that the tract project is approximately
2,900 fee! away fron the property in which you have an econornic
interest.z 
For projects that are Dore than 2,500 feet away from
your real property interest, the effect of the decision generally
will not be considered naterial. 
(Regul.ation 18702.3(b).) The
effect of the decision will be considered material onlv if:
(1) There are specific circumstances
regarding the decision, its effect, and the nature
of the reaL property in which the official 
has an
interest, which nake it reasonably foreseeable that
the fair market value or the rental value of the
real property in which the official 
has an interest
r,ilL be affected by the anounts set forth in
subdivisions (a) (3) (A) or (a) (3) (B); and
(2) Either of the following apply:
(A) The effect will not be substantially
the sane as the effect upon at least 25
percent of all the properties which are lrithin
a 2,5OO foot radius of the boundarj-es of the
reaL property in which the official 
has an
interest; or
2 
rh. commission does not act as a finder of fact. The advice
provided in this letter is applicable only to the extent that the
facts provided are correct and that all of the naterial facts have
been provided. (fn re Oqlesbv (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71, copy en-
closed. )


Fi■e No. A-90-245
Page 5
(B) Ttrere are not at least 10 properties
under separate ownership within a 2,5oo foot
radius of the property in which the official
has an interest.
(Regnrlation La702.3 (b) . )
Under this regulation, you are perDitted to participate in the
decision concerning the tract parcel, unless the value of the
option property will be affected by the dollar amounts specified
in Regulation 18702.3(b) and there are specific factors which roake
the effect on the option property somewhat unique.
We nust leave to you the factual deternination of the
uagnitude of the financial inpact on the option property as a
result of the pending council decision on the tract property and
whether the inpact falls within the guidelines provided in
Regulation Ia102.3 (b) (2) .
If you conclude that the decision will not inpact the option
property's fair narket value by 910,000, or its rental value by
$1,000 in a 12-month period, you are perEitted to participate in
the tract parcel decision. ff you conclude that such a financial
impact is Iikely to result, you are perrnitted to participate in
the decision on the tract parcel provided at least 25 percent of
all the properties within 2,500 feet of the option property will
be affected by the decision in the saDe nanner as the option
property. 3
For exanple, if the option property hrere the onLy parcel
within a 2,5OO foot radius that lras suitable for development
sinilar to the tract proposed by the developer, you uould be
required to disqualify yourself fron participati.ng in the tract
parcel decision because less than the requisite number of
properties within a 2,500- foot radius of the option propertv
would be affected by the tract parcel decision in a nanner sinilar
to the option property. Sinilarly, disgualification would be
required if there were ten parceJ.s within a 2,500-foot radius of
the option property and only one of the ten would be affected by
the tract parcel decision in a nanner sinilar to the option
property. If, however, there rrere twenty properties within a
2,500-foot radj.us of the option property and at Least five of the
twenty lrou1d be affected by the tract parcel decision in a nanner
sinilar to the inpact on the option property, you would then be
pernitted to participate in the tract parcel decision.
3    As yOu have indicated that
under separate ownership within
this wou■d appear to satisfy the
■8702.3(b)(2)(B)。
there are at least ten properties
2,500 feet of the option property,
requirernent of Regulation


File No. A-9o-245
Page 6
And, as noted previousLy, if you are permitted to participate
in the decision concerning the tract parcel, you rrould also be
pernitted to participate in the decisions concerning the
developer,s tlro other properties.
I trugt this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested. If you have any further questions regarding this nat-
ter please contact ne at (915) 322-5091.
sincerely,
Kathryn E. Donovan
KED: JSR: p1h
Enclosures
Legal Division
